docs.title: Student Microelectronics Foundry
docs.text:
The Student Microelectronics Foundry (SMF) is a student-organized platform for practical microelectronics, semiconductor fabrication and microsystems engineering.

The documentation collects:

  • organizational information;
  • safety documentation;
  • fabrication processes;
  • equipment instructions;
  • experiments and student projects.

docs.title: Main areas
docs.text:
• Semiconductor device fabrication
  • Photolithography and microstructuring
  • Doping, oxidation, etching and metallization
  • Electrical characterization
  • Open-source laboratory equipment
  • Teaching and student projects

Documentation is not authorization

Laboratory work may only be performed after the required safety training, equipment instruction and approval.

docs.title: Equipment
docs.text: This section contains documentation for instruments, machines and self-developed laboratory systems.

docs.title: Equipment categories
docs.text:
• Lithography systems
  • Spin coaters
  • Hotplates and furnaces
  • Microscopes
  • Probe stations
  • Measurement equipment
  • Wet-chemical equipment
  • Open-source laboratory equipment

Each equipment page should contain:

  • purpose;
  • location;
  • responsible person;
  • required training;
  • basic operating procedure;
  • maintenance information;
  • known problems;
  • related processes and safety documents.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: This section explains how to use and contribute to the SMF documentation.

docs.title: Reading the documentation
docs.text:
Use the navigation menu or search function to find:

  • processes;
  • equipment;
  • safety information;
  • projects;
  • organizational documents.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Documentation can be edited directly through GitHub.

  1. Open the relevant page.
  2. Select the edit icon.
  3. Make the proposed change.
  4. Create a pull request.
  5. Wait for review and approval.

New contributors should begin with small corrections or pages related to their own projects.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: The SMF documentation should be clear, reproducible and easy to review.

docs.title: Basic rules
docs.text:
• Use descriptive headings.
  • Keep pages focused on one topic.
  • State units and process parameters.
  • Document assumptions and deviations.
  • Add references where appropriate.
  • Link related processes and equipment.
  • Do not publish passwords, personal data or confidential information.
  • Safety-related changes require review.

docs.title: Simple page structure
docs.text:
# Page title

## Purpose

Explain what the page describes.

## Description

Provide the necessary background information.

## Procedure

1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step

## Results

Document observations or measurements.

## References

- Manual, paper or other source


## File names

Use lowercase file names with hyphens:

photolithography.md
spin-coater.md
pn-junction-process.md

docs.title: Organization
docs.text: This section contains information about the organization and development of the Student Microelectronics Foundry.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Mission and objectives
  • Roles and responsibilities
  • Meetings and decisions
  • Purchasing and inventory
  • Finances and funding
  • Partnerships and cooperation
  • Development roadmap

Administrative documents containing personal, financial or confidential information should not be stored in the public documentation repository.

docs.title: Fabrication processes
docs.text: This section documents the fabrication and characterization processes used in the SMF.

docs.title: Initial processes
docs.text:
• Wafer cleaning
  • Photolithography
  • Thermal oxidation
  • Doping
  • Wet and dry etching
  • Metallization
  • Annealing
  • Electrical characterization

Each process page should describe:

  • purpose;
  • required equipment;
  • required materials;
  • important parameters;
  • process steps;
  • expected results;
  • common problems;
  • related safety documents.

Only approved procedures should be marked as established SMF processes.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: This section documents current and completed SMF projects.

docs.title: Initial projects
docs.text:
• PN junction fabrication
  • Semiconductor diodes
  • Solar cells
  • Metal-gate MOS transistors
  • Maskless lithography
  • Open-source spin coater
  • Lithography systems
  • Electrical probe station

docs.title: Suggested project structure
docs.text:
projects/
└── project-name/
    ├── index.md
    ├── process.md
    └── results.md

A project page should briefly describe:

  • objective;
  • responsible contributors;
  • current status;
  • methods;
  • results;
  • open tasks;
  • related documents.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: This section contains the central safety documentation of the SMF.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
• Laboratory rules
  • Required training
  • Equipment instructions
  • Risk assessments
  • Chemical register
  • Safety data sheets
  • Emergency procedures
  • Waste disposal

Controlled documentation

Safety documentation must be reviewed and approved by the responsible university personnel.

Warning

This website does not replace official safety instructions, risk assessments or practical training.
